fields.name: warmup time:seconds#time before the equipment truly becomes active
duration:seconds#use negative numbers for infinite duration

cooldown time:seconds#for equipment that suppresses combat actions, these actions remain suppressed for this long after the equipment deactivates
Note that if this equipment also readies weapons when deactivated, the weapon will not be usable until both this timer expires and the ready animation has finished, whichever is longer
phantom volume activation time#time in seconds for the phantom volumes on this object to start accelerating things in them
energy recovery time:seconds#after deactivation, energy stays constant for this long before starting to change by 'inactive energy rate'
Energy
fields.definition: All these fields are additive, meaning a negative number will reduce energy and a positive one will increase energy.  Every piece of equipment starts at 1.0 energy

fields.name:
minimum activation energy#energy must be at least this high to activate
Like activation cost, but doesn't actually change energy levels
charges#-1 means unlimited charges, 0 means fire on creation

object noise adjustment#for AI perception.
while active, adjusts noises made by the owner unit by this many 'notches'.
note that this is additive, so a positive number is louder and a negative number is quieter

unit stow marker name#the marker on the unit to attach this equipment to when it is stowed.
The equipment should have a marker named "equipment_stow_anchor"
forced primary change color#sets the primary change color on the unit to this if the flag above is checked
forced secondary change color#sets the secondary change color on the unit to this if the flag above is checked
AI
fields.definition: Settings which the AI use to know how they should react to this equipment

fields.name: danger radius#How large a danger zone we should create around this equipment (0 means no danger zone)
min deployment distance:wus#How far does my target have to be for me to throw this at them?
awareness time:seconds#How long I should go unnoticed by nearby enemies
ai dialogue equipment type#The equipment type name used by the ai dialog system used to filter equipment activation dialogue events.
activation while disabled by player traits sound
look acceleration:deg/s/s#if non-zero, limits the change in look velocity per second while the user is pushing the look stick in the current direction of looking
look deceleration:deg/s/s#if non-zero, limits the change in look velocity per second while the user is not pushing the look stick or changing directions

look acc smoothing fraction#if non-zero, when the desired velocity change is less than this fraction of the acceleration, starts interpolating the maximum acceleration towards zero.
You can think of this as a time in seconds where if the velocity would reach its target in this amount of time or less, it will start taking longer.
override fov#if non-zero, overrides the FOV set in the unit or globals
cylinder fraction#controls how far from the focus point the outer raycasts start.  0 == cone, 1 == cylinder
obstruction test angle:degrees#how wide of a cone to test
obstruction max inward accel:1.0/s/s#how quickly the camera moves inward when it anticipates a collision
obstruction max outward accel:1.0/s/s#how quickly the camera returns to a normal position when its anticipated distance is further than its current
obstruction max velocity:1.0/s#maximum speed the camera can move
obstruction return delay:s#when the camera wants to start moving back out, wait this long before doing so

maximum camera velocity:wu/s#how quickly the camera can move to a new displacement (if the velocity suddenly changes).
During this time the aim vector for the unit will be inaccurate, so don't set this too low.
0 defaults to infinite.

fields.definition:
For each of the following functions, if 1 instance of the block is specified, the offsets are symmetric.
If 2 instances are specified, then the first instance is used for positive displacement and the second is used for negative displacement.

fields.name:
maximum value#for linear velocity; this is wu/s
for linear acceleration; this is the fraction of the seat acceleration
for angular velocity; this is deg/s
camera scale (axial)#scale factor used when this acceleration component is along the axis of the forward vector of the camera
camera scale (perpendicular)#scale factor used when this acceleration component is perpendicular to the camera
peg threshold [0,1]#magnitude of analog input for pegged acceleration to kick in
pegged time#time for a pegged look to reach maximum effect
pegged scale#the maximum effect achieved over the duration of the pegged time.
peg max angular velocity:degrees per sec#the maximum turning speed during peg
spawn radius#distance from players eyeball on the z-plane that this effect spawns
spawn z offset#z-offset of effect spawn
spawn area radius#need a sphere of radius r's free space in order to spawn, otherwise we pick a new spawn location
arm time#time before it becomes a proximity mine
self destruct time#seconds after it is created that it must self destruct.  0 means never destroy
trigger time#seconds object moving at trigger velocity takes to trigger explosion.  This will smooth out sensitivity to velocity noise
trigger velocity#WU/sec at which we trigger explosion
arm time#time before it starts making noise
noise radius#radius in WU that the noise extends to.
noise count#number of noise points that are generated
flash radius#radius in WU that the damage flash noise extends to.
showme radius#radius in WU that the showme extends to.
shield recharge rate:1.0f/s#while active, shields recharge at this fraction per second
shield max recharge level#highest level shield can recharge to (can be up to 4)
loop-invincibility shield name#active while the equipment is in use (used for shield rendering effects)
post-invincibility shield name#active once the equipment is no longer in use
post-invincibility time to shield level function
maximum vertical velocity:WU/SEC#we use this to specify the domain of the active vertical velocity funtion
threshold effects#the effect with the highest threshold will play on deactivation
threshold energy burned^:0-1#how much energy you have to burn to play this effect
energy adjustment:-1 to 1#how much energy to add when playing this effect

initial primary weapon#name of an element from global starting weapons block. Other values:
(empty) - unchanged
unchanged - unchanged
default - take from starting profiles in scenario
none - no weapon
random - use random item from valid starting weapons
jump multiplier#use -1 for 'unchanged.'  0 will make the player unable to jump. 100 is default.
Appearance Traits
fields.definition: Traits that affect the player's appearance

fields.name: Motion Sensor Traits
fields.definition: Traits that affect the HUD motion sensor behavior

fields.name: Appearance Traits
fields.definition: Traits that affect the AI's appearance

fields.name:
shield damage to energy scale#scale factor for energy gain from shield damage to child.
Damage is normalized, so if this value is -1.0 then the equipment will lose all its energy when its child's shield is depleted.
negative velocity acceleration scale#when falling, scale the thrust by this (so you can 'catch' yourself easier)
grounded acceleration magnitude#how hard to kick you upwards if you activate while grounded
landing anticipation time:s#how many seconds before landing the jetpack user enters the airborne arc
shimmer decrease rate:1.0/s#how fast shimmer decreases
shimmer bullet ping:0-1#how much to ping shimmer when hit by a bullet

shimmer to camo function#this is a periodic function with a period of 1 second
the shimmer value is used as the range input (interpolates between green and red)

options:
never dropped by ai#if an actor dies while carrying this, it gets deleted immediately
does not affect dropping by players
cannot be active while airborne#if checked, this equipment cannot be activated if the user is airborne, and deactivates itself if the user becomes airborne
cannot activate while airborne#can't activate in midair, but doesn't turn off if you later become airborne
cannot activate while standing on biped#if you are standing on another biped you can not use this equipment
cannot be active in vehicle#can't be activated in a seat, and deactivates if a vehicle is entered
suppresses weapons while active#firing your weapon turns off equipment
suppresses melee while active#meleeing turns off equipment
suppresses grenades while active#throwing a grenade turns off equipment

readies weapon on deactivation#note - if this equipment has an animation cycle, the weapon is readied after the exit animation finishes.  Otherwise the weapon-ready happens immediately on deactivation
hides weapon on activation#checking this flag will automatically cause the weapon to ready on deactivation
can not be picked up by player
does_not_activate_from_prediction#used for evade to ensure that the equipment always activates from an event (more reliable).
toggle#toggles state when X is pressed
hold#activates when X is pressed and deactivates when X is released
hides player-unit from camera#the player controlling this camera will not see their unit.  All other cameras will see this unit

use aiming vector instead of marker forward#for cameras without tracks that use a marker position, specifies that we use the unit's aiming vector instead of the marker's forward vector.
This results in more accurate aiming and smoother movement when frames are dropped
moving friendly bipeds moving neutral vehicles
